title: Windows Search Index Repair
description: Diagnose Windows Search/indexing failures, restart Search only when approved, and verify service health.
starter_prompt: Fix Windows search or indexing problems with approval before service changes.

phases.prompt: Scope the search/indexing symptom and collect evidence before restarting services.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Search service'; Get-Service WSearch -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Status,StartType,CanStop | Format-List; Section 'Search process'; Get-Process SearchIndexer,SearchProtocolHost,SearchFilterHost -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ProcessName,Id,CPU,WorkingSet64 | Format-Table -AutoSize; Section 'Search events'; Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='Application'; ProviderName='Microsoft-Windows-Search'; StartTime=(Get-Date).AddDays(-14)} -MaxEvents 120 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object TimeCreated,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message | Format-List

phases.prompt: Collect read-only Windows Search service, process, and event evidence.
Analyze Search evidence and decide whether restart/reindex guidance is justified. Call out missing event evidence.
Ask for explicit approval before restarting Windows Search. Offer restore point handling for service/system changes.
phases.completion: Approval and restore point decision are recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output 'Restarting Windows Search after approval.'; Restart-Service WSearch -Force -ErrorAction Stop; Get-Service WSearch | Select-Object Name,Status,StartType | Format-List

phases.prompt: Run only after approval and restore point offer. Restart Windows Search and capture final service status.
phases.completion: Approved service restart completed or failure is recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Get-Service WSearch -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Status,StartType | Format-List; Get-Process SearchIndexer -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ProcessName,Id,CPU,WorkingSet64 | Format-List

phases.prompt: Verify Windows Search service/process state after any approved action.
Check that service restart was justified and that deeper index reset is left as a separate approved step.

Create a technician and customer-ready handoff report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized follow-up next actions.
Keep logs and reports. Remove only workflow-created scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Search evidence is collected before service restart.
Service restart runs only after approval.
Final report includes evidence, actions, cleanup status, risks, and follow-up items.
